title: Recipe for standard BG-11 media
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3suc3","text":"Stanier RY, Deruelles J, Rippka R, Herdman M, Waterbury JB: Generic Assignments, Strain Histories and Properties of Pure Cultures of Cyanobacteria. Microbiology 1979, 111:1–61.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":60,"length":87}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dsbhh","text":"Recipes for standard and alternative BG11 for culturing freshwater cyanobacteria, such as Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803, as described.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":90,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7i89s","text":"Media is usually not suitable for marine cyanobacteria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9jkvu","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dhoa7","text":"Always work under sterile conditions when handling sterile media or stocks. Work under the clean bench.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7802m","text":"Wear gloves when preparing stocks!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c8uh1","text":"Heavy metals are toxic for the environment and need to be discarded accordingly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: CaCl 2 2H 2 O (3.6 g · L -1 ) Citric acid (0.6 g · L -1 ) NaNO 3 (149.58 g · L -1 ) MgSO 4 · 7 H 2 O (7.49 g · L -1 ) 0.25 M Na 2 -EDTA, pH 8.0 (0.56 ml · L -1 ) For 100x BG11 Stock -N: Omit NaNO 3 .

1000x Na 2 CO 3 : 20 mg L -1 100x TES-buffer, pH 8.0 (1M), adjust with KOH 1000x K 2 HPO 4 x 3 H 2 O: 30 mg · mL -1 1000x Fe(III) ammonium citrate (6 mg · L -1 ) 5000x CuSO 4 5 H 2 O (395 ng · mL -1 ) (sterilize using a filter)

1000x concentration: H 3 BO 3 (2.86 g · L -1 ) MnCl 2 · 4 H 2 O (1.81 g · L -1 ) ZnSO 4 · 7 H 2 O (0.222 g · L -1 ) Na 2 MoO 4 · 2 H 2 O (0.390 g · L -1 ) Co(NO 3 ) 2 · 6 H 2 O (0.049 g · L -1 ) For BG11 lacking certain metals (e.g. for working with metal inducible promoters P petE , P coaT , P ziaA etc., trace metal mix can be prepared lacking these chemicals and used instead of standard trace metal mix.

Fill 1 L bottle with 500 mL ultra pure water. Add stock solutions as shown below. %s %s Stock solution Volume 100x BG11 Stock 10 mL 1000x Na2CO3 1 mL 1000x K2HPO4 x 3 H2O 1 mL 100x TES-buffer 10 mL 1000x Trace Metal Mix 1 mL Add ultra pure water to 1 L. Autoclave. After autoclaving, add 1 mL 1000x Fe(III) ammonium citrate. Optional: After autoclaving, add 200 µL 5000x CuSO 4

Fill 1 L bottle with 500 mL ultra pure water. Add stock solutions as shown below. %s %s Stock solution Volume 100x BG11 Stock -N 10 mL 1000x Na2CO3 1 mL 1000x K2HPO4 x 3 H2O 1 mL 100x TES-buffer 10 mL 1000x Trace Metal Mix 1 mL Add ultra pure water to 1 L. Autoclave. After autoclaving, add 1 mL sterile 1000x Fe(III) ammonium citrate. Optional: After autoclaving, add 200 µL sterile 5000x CuSO 4

Fill 500 mL bottle with 250 mL ultra pure water. Add stock solutions as shown below. %s %s Stock solution Volume 100x BG11 Stock -N 10 mL 1000x Na2CO3 1 mL 1000x K2HPO4 x 3 H2O 1 mL 100x TES-buffer, pH = 8.0 10 mL 1000x Trace Metal Mix 1 mL Add ultra pure water to 500 mL. Autoclave. After autoclaving, add 1 mL sterile 1000x Fe(III) ammonium citrate. Optional: After autoclaving, add 200 µL sterile 5000x CuSO 4
Prepare 1.5 % agar: Weigh 4.5 g Bacto Agar. Fill up to 300 mL. Autoclave. Microwave agar until liquid. Let cool.
In a 50 mL Falcon, add 1 vol 2x BG11 and 1 vol liquid 1.5 % agar. (Note: Usually, one plate requires 30-40 mL total volume.)
When mixture is hand warm, add appropriate antibiotics, if required. Quickly pour plate, avoiding air bubbl
creator: Anna Behle
